predecessor: F-THERMO1 (L-1418, S514) — domain Boltzmann k values
prediction: Domain Boltzmann k predicts compaction effectiveness: positive-k (diversifying) domains accumulate more orphan/low-cited lessons, requiring more compaction. |r(k, compaction_metric)| > 0.3 for at least one compaction metric.
falsification_criterion: |r(k, compaction_metric)| < 0.3 for ALL compaction metrics (zero_cited_frac, low_cited_frac, compaction_savings_pct)
method: Used domains/ INDEX.md to map lessons to domains (same method as F-THERMO1, N=8 domains with 11-23 lessons each). For each domain: counted zero-cited orphans, low-cited (<=2) lessons, computed citation Gini, vocab diversity, avg tokens, cite density. Correlated each metric against domain Boltzmann k.

k_vs_zero_cited_frac.interpretation: No relationship. Only filtering domain has orphans (2/13), and its k is near zero.

k_vs_low_cited_frac.interpretation: Identical to zero_cited — low-cited lessons are the same as zero-cited in this sample.

k_vs_avg_tokens.interpretation: Weak positive trend. Positive-k domains write slightly longer lessons, but not significant.

k_vs_avg_citations.interpretation: No relationship between k and citation count.

k_vs_citation_gini.interpretation: No relationship. Citation inequality is not predicted by k.

k_vs_vocab_diversity.interpretation: SECONDARY FINDING: Positive-k domains have higher vocabulary diversity (r=0.63, borderline significant). Diversifying domains use more unique words per lesson. This is consistent with the thermodynamic interpretation but does not predict compaction.

k_vs_compaction_savings_pct.interpretation: All domains except filtering have 0% compaction savings. No variance to correlate.

detail: Domain Boltzmann k does NOT predict compaction need. All primary compaction metrics (zero_cited_frac, low_cited_frac, savings_pct) show r < 0.15. The domain-indexed lessons (N=11-23 per domain) are well-cited across the board — 7/8 domains have zero orphan lessons. Compaction need is driven by lesson age and structural position, not domain thermodynamics. Secondary finding: k vs vocab_diversity r=0.63 (p~0.05) confirms F-THERMO1's interpretation that positive-k domains diversify linguistically, but this vocabulary effect does not translate into compaction opportunity.
why_falsified: Domain-indexed lessons are heavily curated (listed in domains/ INDEX.md), creating survivorship bias — orphans are unlikely to be domain-indexed
Compaction is driven by age and citation network position (Sharpe = citations/age), not domain membership
The 8 F-THERMO1 domains represent only the best-documented domains; compaction-heavy domains may not have enough lessons for Boltzmann fitting
Current system drift is 4.5% (healthy) — there is little compaction pressure overall, reducing variance in compaction metrics

secondary_finding.interpretation: Positive-k domains (strategy k=+0.15, meta k=+0.05) use more diverse vocabulary than negative-k domains (nk-complexity k=-0.18, vocab=0.44). This validates F-THERMO1's thermodynamic interpretation: positive-k = entropy-increasing = diversifying vocabulary; negative-k = entropy-decreasing = vocabulary convergence. nk-complexity has lowest vocab diversity (0.44) and most negative k (-0.18), consistent with a domain that has converged on a shared technical vocabulary.

implications: Compaction strategy should remain domain-agnostic — domain k does not identify compaction targets
compact.py's Sharpe-based ranking (citation/age) is the correct compaction signal, not domain thermodynamics

The k-vocab_diversity correlation (r=0.63) suggests domain k captures real structural differences in knowledge organization, but these differences manifest as vocabulary structure, not as compaction need

Negative-k domains self-organize vocabulary (convergent terminology) while positive-k domains explore vocabulary (divergent terminology) — this is a linguistic fingerprint of the thermodynamic constant
Future work: test whether vocab diversity predicts lesson merge-ability (semantic overlap between lessons within a domain)
caveats: N=8 domains limits statistical power (df=6 for correlations)
Domain-indexed lessons have survivorship bias (well-integrated lessons are more likely to be indexed)
Only 1 domain (filtering) has any zero-cited lessons, making compaction metrics degenerate
System currently at 4.5% drift (healthy) — compaction metrics would be richer during high-drift periods
p-values are approximate (analytic approximation, not exact t-distribution)
